methodology: Self-claims about Origa verified against README.md, AGENTS.md, marketing/origa-seo.factcheck.json, marketing/product-hunt.md (direct read)
Competitor / ecosystem claims verified against cited URLs in raw research files (marketing/research/2026-07-18-*.md)
Predictions (Origa-уроки, talking points) explicitly marked as PREDICTIONS, not facts — confidence 0.6-0.8
Reddit .json endpoints NOT used (dead since May 2026 per system prompt); all Reddit quotes sourced via Tavily search engine indexing

claims.claim: Origa is built on Rust + Leptos 0.8 + Tauri v2 stack.
claims.note: AGENTS.md explicitly states: Frontend = Leptos 0.8, Desktop = Tauri v2, Business logic in Rust.

claims.claim: Origa uses FSRS (Free Spaced Repetition Scheduler), same algorithm family as Anki.
claims.note: rs-fsrs is the Rust port of FSRS, the same algorithm Anki 24.x defaults to.

claims.claim: Origa uses NDLOCR for OCR and Whisper for STT, both running locally.
sources: AGENTS.md Key dependencies: ort + NDLOCR-Lite (OCR), ort + rustfft (Whisper STT)
claims.note: Both ML models run on-device via ort (ONNX Runtime). No cloud calls for OCR/STT.

claims.claim: Origa uses lindera + UniDic for Japanese tokenization.
Origa is licensed under BSL 1.1 (Business Source License), which is NOT OSI-approved open source.
claims.note: BSL is source-available but NOT in the OSI-approved list. README correctly describes restrictions.

claims.claim: marketing/product-hunt.md line 41 contains the phrase 'The app is free and open-source' which is factually incorrect for BSL 1.1.
claims.note: Flagged as BLOCKER B1. This wording must be corrected before launch — any dev-savvy user checking LICENSE will register trust hit.

claims.claim: Origa landing serves 4 locales (EN/RU/KO/VI).
claims.note: Previously verified 2026-06-26.

claims.claim: Origa has 200,000+ native Japanese phrases built in.
sources: README.md:57 ('200,000+ phrases from native Japanese content')

claims.claim: Leptos creator gbj announced in May 2026 that Leptos is 'feature-complete' and will be 'lightly maintained going forward, no significant new development'.
claims.note: Snapshot 2026-07-18. The Leptos repo and Issue #4707 are publicly accessible; the quoted text matches the research summary.

claims.claim: NDLOCR-Lite achieves CER 0.016 (1.6%) on printed documents, 0.323 on books, 0.268 on handwriting.
sources: https://estyle.co.jp/media/エンジニアアログ/2973/ (cited in research)
claims.note: Benchmarks from Japanese-language technical blog post. Snapshot 2026-07-18. The 1.6% CER figure is the one most relevant to Origa's use case (manga panels, textbook photos).

claims.claim: Whisper large-v3-turbo achieves 21.8% CER on Japanese; Google Chirp 3 achieves 6.4% CER.
claims.note: Two independent benchmark sources cited in research. Snapshot 2026-07-18. Numbers vary by test set; these are representative.

claims.claim: Migaku raised lifetime price from $200 to $400 to announced $500 within ~18 months; also raised prices during user trials.
claims.note: Two independent sources (WaniKani Community + Reddit) corroborate the pricing escalation timeline. The 'raised prices during my 10-day trial' quote is from r/LearnJapanese.

claims.claim: jpdb.io had an undocumented 10K deck cap that caused user data loss in 2024.
claims.note: Single primary source (WaniKani Community thread). User reported reaching 9999 vocab and accidentally deleting deck trying to move cards.

claims.claim: Reddit .json endpoints have been dead since May 2026; all research used Tavily search + Playwright + PRAW instead.
sources: @marketer system prompt: 'Reddit .json endpoints МЕРТВЫ. С мая 2026 Reddit закрыл публичные .json endpoints'
claims.note: Confirmed by system prompt. This is a hard constraint, not a research finding.

claims.claim: HashiCorp's 2023 MPL→BSL switch caused OpenTofu fork (33K stars in a month) and contributor exodus (21.12% → 9.30% community PRs).
claims.note: Multiple secondary sources. The 21.12%→9.30% community PR figure is from a single retrospective blog; directionally reliable.

claims.claim: Tauri v2 production retrospectives show 22% more native bugs vs Electron, 70% platform-specific WebView bugs.
claims.note: From a '6 months, 3 apps' production retrospective cited in research. Single source but plausible given WebView fragmentation.

claims.claim: Manabi Reader HN launch received 105 points, 57 comments; positive reception driven by offline-first + Anki integration + known-in-community author.
claims.note: HN points count is snapshot-dependent. The reception pattern (offline-first + Anki integration = positive) is the actionable insight.

claims.claim: Origa uses Whisper locally without VAD preprocessing (specific to 'objection #12 — gap' framing).
claims.note: [CITATION NEEDED] — Origa's STT implementation details (VAD preprocessing, model size, Whisper variant) are not in README/AGENTS.md. The talking point in objection #12 assumes Whisper's standard configuration; if Origa uses VAD-preprocessed Whisper or a fine-tuned variant, the actual JP CER may be lower. Recommend: check origa/src/stt/ or origa_ui/src/repository/ for STT implementation before publishing objection #12 talking point.

claims.claim: Origa supports Anki .apkg import and export (referenced in objections #1, #6, #9 talking points).
claims.note: [CITATION NEEDED] — Neither README.md nor AGENTS.md explicitly confirms .apkg import/export. The README mentions 'Import ready-made word sets from other popular apps' (line 38) but doesn't specify Anki format. BLOCKER B3 in playbook asks user to verify this before launch. If unsupported, objections #1, #6, #9 talking points must be reworded to drop the .apkg claim.

claims.claim: Bunpro has poor SRS quality compared to FSRS — specifically short ghost intervals, no leech handling, and pile-up at small intervals.
claims.note: Single primary source (Bunpro Community). Multiple users in the same thread corroborate the same complaints.

claims.claim: WaniKani enforces a strict learning order with no early reviews and no ease system.
claims.note: User-reported in WaniKani Community. Multiple users in different threads confirm the same restriction.

claims.claim: Migii JLPT app has documented issues with incorrect answers in tests and poor audio quality.
claims.note: Multiple App Store reviews corroborate. App Store doesn't expose review dates in web view.

claims.claim: Yomitan (Yomichan fork) is the de facto Japanese pop-up dictionary; 2687+ GitHub stars; users complain about dictionary setup friction.
claims.note: Yomitan's own dev team acknowledged the setup friction problem in a public r/LearnJapanese post.

claims.claim: Animecards/QM author was attacked on 4chan /jp/ with 'He's Russian' as a vector.
claims.note: [CITATION NEEDED] — Single secondary reference in research summary; no direct 4chan URL captured (4chan threads are ephemeral). Pattern is plausible given 4chan /jp/ documented toxicity, but the specific case is not independently verifiable.

claims.claim: MaruMori is positioned as 'NO AI' — all content hand-crafted by humans.
claims.note: Launch announcement explicitly contrasts MaruMori's hand-crafted approach with AI-generated content. Not directly relevant to Origa positioning but contextually important.

claims.claim: Origa has ~500+ tests covering the domain layer (referenced in objection #14 talking point).
claims.note: [CITATION NEEDED] — Approximate figure referenced in objection #14. AGENTS.md mentions 'cargo test --workspace' as a command but no specific test count. The 500+ figure is a placeholder — should be replaced with actual count from `cargo test --workspace 2>&1 | grep 'test result'` before publishing the talking point.

predictions.claim: Top 5 objections Origa will face on launch are: #1 'Yet another Anki alternative', #2 'BSL is not OSS', #3 'Leptos maintenance mode', #5 'Migaku already does this', #6 'data portability'.
predictions.note: Based on patterns observed in research. Not a fact about future — a structured prediction. Confidence 0.8 = strong pattern match but actual reception may differ.

predictions.claim: Origa's positioning as 'all-in-one Japanese learning app' will trigger 'Migaku parallel' objections in 70%+ of HN/Reddit launch threads.
predictions.note: Pattern from Migaku/MaruMori/Kitsun launches. 'All-in-one' is a structural trigger phrase in this community.

predictions.claim: Talking points in §2 are brand-voice compliant and will land with the target audience.
predictions.note: Cannot verify without field testing. Talking points follow Builder-Architect / Pragmatic Operator / Precision Educator blend and avoid forbidden words. Real reception requires actual community testing.

flags: BLOCKER B1 (product-hunt.md:41 'free and open-source' wording) — must fix before launch
[CITATION NEEDED] Origa Whisper configuration — verify before publishing objection #12 talking point
[CITATION NEEDED] Anki .apkg import/export support — verify before publishing objections #1, #6, #9 talking points
[CITATION NEEDED] Test count (~500+) — replace with actual count before publishing objection #14 talking point
[CITATION NEEDED] 4chan /jp/ 'Russian author' attack vector — single secondary reference, not directly verifiable
Pricing model (objection #15) — gap, decision required before launch
All PREDICTION claims are explicitly predictions, not facts; overall_confidence (0.86) reflects verification of factual claims only
gate_note: Gate is READY_FOR_REVIEW (not READY_FOR_PUBLISH) because: (1) three BLOCKERS (B1/B2/B3) must be resolved before any launch copy is published, (2) four [CITATION NEEDED] items require user/code verification, (3) HUMAN GATE is pending — user was absent during autonomous run. Awaiting explicit user approval or revision before any artifact is used for launch materials.
